article.description: — Franz Wagner scored 31 points, Paolo Banchero had 28 points, 13 rebounds and seven assists and the Orlando Magic beat the Washington Wizards 130-125 Friday night to tie a franchise record with their ninth straight victory.
article.headline: Wagner, Banchero lead Magic to win over Wizards for ninth straight victory

images.caption: Paolo Banchero scores 28 points in the Magic's 130-125 win over the Wizards, tying Orlando's franchise record with its ninth straight win.
images.name: Banchero drops 28 points as Magic tie franchise-record win streak

article.linkText: Wagner, Banchero lead Magic to win over Wizards for ninth straight victory
article.story: ORLANDO, Fla. -- — Franz Wagner scored 31 points, Paolo Banchero had 28 points, 13 rebounds and seven assists and the Orlando Magic beat the Washington Wizards 130-125 Friday night to tie a franchise record with their ninth straight victory. It was the third straight 30-point game for Franz Wagner, whose brother Moritz Wagner added 18 points to help Orlando beat Washington for the second time in three nights. The Magic have had four nine-game winning streaks, previously in 1994, 2001 and 2010-11. They are one of only two NBA franchises without a double-digit win streak on their record. The Indiana Pacers’ longest win streak is also nine games. “It's not easy to stack up wins in this league,” noted Moritz Wagner. “It’s pretty special,” Banchero said. “Eventually I think a loss is going to come but we just want to keep it rolling for as long as we can. But you’re not surprised when you do something like this because we’ve got great chemistry and we play really well together.” The Magic will try to set the new franchise record Saturday night at Brooklyn, site of their last defeat on Nov. 14. “It’s big time for this group, but more importantly I think it’s big-time for the fans and the organization,” said coach Jamahl Mosley. “There’s an energy, there’s a vibe, a buzz about this group, and our guys are doing a good job of embracing it -- not getting too high or too low but being able to register that this is something special.” Kyle Kuzma scored 27 points and Daniel Gafford had 18 points and 11 rebounds for the Wizards, who have lost 11 of 12. Kuzma, who fouled out in the final minute, left the two-game set a big believer. “Look at Franz and look at Paolo,” he said. “They can manipulate the game because they are so big, so strong, and just (play) downhill. They are a problem.” Washington made seven of its first nine 3-point attempts (three by Corey Kispert) and led throughout most of the first half. The game turned in Orlando's favor midway through the third quarter when Jalen Suggs and Banchero combined for 10 straight points and Kuzma and Gafford were whistled for their fourth personal fouls. But the Magic did not pull ahead for good until Franz Wagner's layup with 7:06 remaining opened a 7-0 run. The Magic had 34 assists on 46 field goals and committed only nine turnovers. Magic forward Jonathan Isaac left the game with a sprained left ankle after getting five rebounds in seven minutes in the first half. UP NEXT Wizards: Host Philadelphia on Wednesday night. Magic: Visit Brooklyn on Saturday night. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/NBA

images.name: Luka Doncic [1296x729]

images.name: LeBron James  [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs handles the ball during the game against the Detroit Pistons on March 5, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs continue to see their championship odds shift.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Jayson Tatum #0 of the Boston Celtics celebrates after scoring against the Dallas Mavericks during the second quarter at TD Garden on March 06, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics' title odds have shortened since Jayson Tatum's return last week.
images.name: Jayson Tatum [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 and Devin Vassell #24 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the New Orleans Pelicans on January 25, 2026 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Led by Victor Wembanyama, the Spurs continue to see their NBA championship odds shorten.
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Even after a rough stretch of games, the Thunder remain the heavy favorites to win the NBA championship.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Cleveland Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell (45) against the Phoenix Suns in the first half at Mortgage Matchup Center.
images.caption: Adding James Harden to a talented roster with the likes of Donovan Mitchell gives the Cavs a higher upside this season.
images.name: Donovan Mitchell [1296x729]

images.alt: Jaylen Brown #7 of the Boston Celtics shoots against the Portland Trail Blazers at TD Garden on January 26, 2026 in Boston, Massachusetts.
images.caption: The Celtics now have shorter odds to win the NBA title than the Spurs, Rockets, Knicks and Pistons.
images.name: Jaylen Brown [1296x729]

images.alt: Jamal Murray #27 of the Denver Nuggets dribbles the ball during the game against the Dallas Mavericks on January 14, 2026 at American Airlines Center in Dallas, Texas.
images.caption: Jamal Murray has led the Nuggets while Nikola Jokic recovers from injury.
images.name: Jamal Murray  [1296x729]

images.alt: Victor Wembanyama #1 of the San Antonio Spurs looks on during the game against the Cleveland Cavaliers on December 29, 2025 at the Frost Bank Center in San Antonio, Texas.
images.caption: Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs have seen their odds to win the NBA championship shorten in the past week.
